name: Virgin Mary
shortDescription: The Virgin Mary, mother of Jesus in the New Testament, is a central figure in Christianity and revered for her purity and role in salvation history. Her story is rooted in Jewish tradition and prophecy.
description: Mary, also known as Saint Mary, Mother Mary, and the Virgin Mary, is revered in Christianity as the mother of Jesus. Her story is primarily told in the New Testament Gospels and the Quran. According to Christian belief, Mary conceived Jesus miraculously through the Holy Spirit while still a virgin, fulfilling prophecies from the Hebrew Bible (Old Testament). Her acceptance of God's will, as told in the Annunciation, is considered a pivotal moment in salvation history. Mary's role extends beyond motherhood; she is seen as a compassionate intercessor and a symbol of purity, faith, and obedience. In Catholic and Orthodox traditions, she is venerated with titles such as 'Mother of God' (Theotokos) and is believed to have been assumed bodily into heaven. The story of Mary is deeply intertwined with Jewish history and Messianic expectations. She is considered a descendant of King David, fulfilling prophecies about the lineage of the Messiah. While the concept of virgin birth is not central to Jewish theology, the narrative of Mary highlights the divine intervention and fulfillment of ancient promises within a Jewish context, leading to the birth of Jesus, whom Christians believe to be the Messiah.
